questionText: The depth of water in a harbour is $D(t) = 6\sin(30t)^\circ + 8\cos(30t)^\circ + 12$ metres, where $t$ is the time in hours after midnight. Using an R-form, find the maximum depth and the values of $t$ in $0 \le t < 12$ for which $D \ge 17$ m.

steps.description: Write the oscillating part in R-form
steps.explanation: Combining the two terms simplifies both the maximum and the inequality.
The amplitude is $10$ and the phase is about $53.13^\circ$.
Substituting the R-form gives a single-sine model.
The maximum occurs when the sine equals $1$.
We want the times when the depth is at least $17$ m.
Subtracting $12$ and dividing by $10$ isolates the sine.
Sine equals $0.5$ at these angles; the sine is at or above $0.5$ between $30^\circ$ and $150^\circ$ in each cycle.
Since $30t + 53.13^\circ$ starts at $53.13^\circ$, the first band where the sine is $\ge 0.5$ runs from $150^\circ$ up to $390^\circ$ (i.e. $30^\circ$ of the next cycle).
Subtracting the phase and dividing by $30$ gives the time window when the depth is at least $17$ m.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: Maximum depth $22$ m; $D \ge 17$ m for approximately $3.23 \le t \le 11.23$ hours.
